source_metadata.title_raw: Lisa A. Travis - A-20-001 - January 27, 2020 - Sacramento

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Lisa A. Travis 
County Counsel of Sacramento 
700 H Street, Suite 2650 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-20-001 
Dear Ms. Travis: 
January 27, 2020 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Sacramento County Supervisor 
Susan Peters regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section 1090. 
Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
QUESTION 
May Supervisor Peters participate in decisions relating to the approval of the Mather South 
Community Master Plan ("Mather South Project"), and accompanying amendments to the County's 
General Plan and Mather Specific Plan where she owns commercial and undeveloped real property 
immediately adjacent to the General and Specific Plan boundary and over 13,000 feet from the 
Mather South Project boundary? 
CONCLUSION 
Based on the facts provided, the decisions solely affect the clearly defined Project area 
located over 13,000 feet from her property and within the larger Mather Field Specific Plan and 
General Plan areas. To the extent that the decisions to approve the Mather South Project, and the 
accompanying amendments to the General Plan and Mather Field Specific Plan and the Mather 
South Urban Services Plan are solely limited in impact to the Mather South Project area, it is not 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-20-001 
Page No. 2 
reasonably foreseeable the decisions will have a material financial effect on Supervisor Peters' 
financial interest under Regulation 18702.2(b ). 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
Supervisor Susan Peters has been a member of the Sacramento County Board of Supervisors 
since 2005. Supervisor Peters owns real property parcels located within the City of Rancho 
Cordova and immediately adjacent to the boundary for the Mather Field Specific Plan and the 
County General Plan. Her two properties are located approximately 13,400 feet from the closest 
boundary of the Mather South Project area. 2 
Mather Field Specific Plan and the Mather South Project 
With the decommission of Mather Air Force Base in the 1990's, the County adopted the 
Mather Field Specific Plan to create a comprehensive plan for reuse of the former base's 5,617-acre 
area. The Mather South Project represents the second step in a two-step planning process for the 
Mather Field Specific Plan Area. The first, the Mather Field Project, modified land uses and was 
completed in September 2016.3 
As currently proposed, the County's decision to approve the Mather South Project would 
result in amendments to the County's General Plan, the Mather Field Specific Plan, and the Mather 
Field Special Planning Area Ordinance and the adoption of a development agreement, as follows: 
County General Plan: 
• 
• 
• 
Land Use Diagram to reflect changing the designation of the Project's 795 acres 
from "Urban Development" to Low Density Residential (622 acres), Medium 
Density Residential (17 acres), Natural Preserve (86 acres) Commercial and Offices 
(42 acres), and Public/Quasi-Public (28 acres). 
Transportation Plan to reflect proposed roadway alignments and transit systems 
within and relating to the Project area. 
Bicycle Master Plan to add internal and external bicycle facilities within and through 
the Project area. 
Mather Field Specific Plan: 
• 
Changing the designation of 795 acres in the Project area from "Urban Development 
Area" to 'Mather South Community Master Plan." 
2 The original request represented that the "properties are located 5,575 feet and 5,430 feet away from the 
closest boundary of the Mather South Project area;" however, the County Planning staff clarified that this measurement 
was to the Illa M. Collin Conservation Preserve boundary, a 1,342 acre site that lies between the properties and the 
Mather South Project. 
3 Although the Mather South Project is a separate project from the Mather Field Project, it is geographically 
contained within the Mather Field Project area. Therefore, the conceptual plan for the Mather South Community Master 
Plan has been included in the Mather Field Project planning efforts. See EIR, Mather South Project Name, p. 1-13 EIR 
viewed on January 7, 2020 at hllps://planning.saccounty.net/PlansandProjectsln-
Progrcss/Documents/Gr01 lh%20Area%20Plans/Mather%20. outh/D • IR%20-%20 I .8. I 9/Draft%20 m. Complete.pdf 


File No. A-20-001 
Page No. 3 
• 
Adoption of the Mather South Community Master Plan, including design guidelines 
and development standards as a part of the Mather Field Specific Plan 
Zoning Ordinance Amendment of the Mather Field Special Planning Area Ordinance 
• 
To incorporate the Mather South Project design guidelines and development 
standards. 
Adoption of a Development Agreement for the Mather South Project 
• 
Between the County of Sacramento and Applicants, with agreements that the 
Applicants will implement benefits to the Mather South Community. 
Mather Field Public Facilities Financing Plan (PFFP) 
• 
To incorporate the Mather South PFFP cost estimates and a strategy for financing the 
infrastructure and public facilities to serve the Project Area. 
The Board of Supervisors will also consider certifying the Project's Environmental Impact 
Report ("EIR") and the approval of the Mather South Urban Services Plan, which will illustrate 
how urban and municipal services such as law enforcement, parks, transit, and operations and 
maintenance of public facilities will be funded and delivered. 
The Mather South Project proposes an 848-acre master planned community with up to 3,522 
residential dwelling units of various densities (multi-family, detached, and attached single-family), 
a 28-acre environmental education campus including 200 multi-family dwelling units, a 21-acre 
research and development park, two elementary schools, a 6-acre community center, 21 acres of 
commercial-retail with up to 225,000 square feet of retail space, 44 acres of parkland including 26 
acres of neighborhood parks and a 17-acre community park, and 210 acres of open space areas that 
include a 53-acre portion of the Mather Preserve west of Zinfandel Drive, as well as other natural 
preserves and drainage corridors, stormwater quality and detention basins, landscape buffers, and 
public utility corridors all connected by multi-use pedestrian and bicycle trails. The project includes 
an Affordable Housing Strategy, which consists ofland dedication of 10 acres in the central portion 
of the Plan Area, which will be used for the construction of 200 affordable housing units. 
Supervisor Peters owns a property located at 10370 and 10390 Peter A. Mccuen Blvd. This 
property consists of a commercial office building whose current tenants are the State of California, 
Office of Emergency Services, Conduent, Inc. and Educational Credit Management Corporation. In 
addition, Supervisor Peters owns nearby undeveloped land bounded by Mather Field Road, Von 
Karmen Street, Armstrong Avenue and Peter A. McCuen Blvd. Supervisor Peters' ownership 
interest in these properties is greater than $2,000. Her properties are not under the jurisdiction of the 
County General Plan or the Mather Specific Plan but are immediately adjacent to these Plan's 
boundaries. The properties are located approximately 13,400 feet away from the closest boundary 
of the Mather South Project. You state that you and Supervisor Peters are unaware of any evidence 
that any decision related the Mather South Project will have a substantial effect on Supervisor 
Peters' property in the City of Rancho Cordova. 


ANALYSIS 
File No. A-20-001 
Page No. 4 
The Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit any public official from making, 
participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental 
decision in which the official has a financial interest. (Section 87100.) A public official has a 
"financial interest" in a governmental decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on 
the public generally, on one or more of the public official's interests. (Section 87103; Regulation 
18700(a).) Section 87103 defines a financial interest to include any real property in which the 
public official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 or more. Supervisor Peters has a 
financial interest in the two real properties identified. 
Where the financial interest is not explicitly involved in a decision, the financial effect is 
reasonably foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic possibility, more than hypothetical or 
theoretical. (Regulation 1870 I (b ). ) Supervisor Peters real property interests are outside the County 
General Plan, Mather Field Specific Plan and the Mather South Project boundaries, and her real 
property is not the subject of the amendment decisions. Under Regulation 1870 I (b ), she will have a 
financial interest in the amendment decisions if it is a realistic possibility the decisions will have a 
material financial effect on her real property interest. 
Supervisor Peters real property is immediately adjacent to the General Plan and the Specific 
Plan involved in the decisions. Under Regulation l 8702.2(a)(7), the effect of a decision involving 
property within 500 feet of the official's real property interest is presumed material unless there is 
clear and convincing evidence that the decision will not have any measurable impact on the 
official's property. Under Regulation l 8702.2(b ), the effect of a decision involving property more 
than 1,000 feet from the official's property is presumed not material unless clear and convincing 
evidence shows the decision would have a substantial effect on the official's property. 
You have provided additional information on the anticipated scope of the proposed County 
General Plan and Mather Field Specific Plan amendments. You state that these amendments 
"consist of land use designation changes that apply only within the Mather South project area, and 
roadway, transit, and bicycle facility designation changes surrounding and within to the Project 
area. Further, you noted that the housing provided by the Mather South Project "represents a portion 
of the County's Regional Housing Needs Allocation, including affordable housing, and does not 
offset a requirement for housing elsewhere in the County or in the larger Mather Field Specific 
Plan." 
Generally, where a project involves a General or Specific Plan, we look to the boundary of 
the General or Specific Plan in determining the distance between official's property and the 
property involved in a decision. However, your facts indicate that these decisions affect the two 
Plans only as to the Mather South Project area, with no other impacts as to the General or Specific 
Plans. Therefore, these decisions "involve property located 1,000 feet or more from the property 
line of the official's property" and the standard in 18702.2(b) is applicable. This is consistent with 
our past advice that measuring to the specific project may be appropriate "where the governmental 
decision affects a clearly defined, specific, and isolated site, such as a particular building on a large 
tract ofland." (Bakker Advice Letter, No. A-06-070; see also McLaughlin Advice Letter, No. A-05-
061.) 


File No. A-20-001 
Page No. 5 
In this case, the amendments to the two Plans appear limited to the Project area, a clearly 
defined site within the very large Mather Field Specific Plan. The Project is located over 13,400 
feet from Supervisor Peters' real property. The Supervisor's properties are separated from the 
Project area by Mather Air Field, zoned industrial areas, a 1,342-acre nature preserve area, and a 
single-family housing district. The proposed changes in the Project area are from a general "urban 
development" designation to more specifically designated mixed densities in housing, with mixed 
uses of natural preserve, commercial and office, and public-type spaces. The amendments 
incorporate changes to the transportation plan and the bicycle plan through, and relating to, the 
Project area. The nature of the proposed changes, geographical distance, and intervening 
commercial and nature areas, relating to these amendments indicate that the decisions will not 
impact the official's property. Therefore, where the decisions solely affect the Project area, and that 
area is separated by 13,400 feet of distance, including Mather Air Field, nature preserves and 
commercial areas, there is no evidence that the amendments will have a substantial effect on her 
real property. 
To the extent that the decisions to approve the Mather South Project, and the accompanying 
amendments to the General Plan and Mather Field Specific Plan and the Mather South Urban 
Services Plan are limited in impact to the Mather South Project area, Supervisor Peters does not 
appear to have a reasonably foreseeable and material financial interest in the decisions. However, 
we caution that where decisions alter the County General Plan or Mather Field Specific Plan and 
have impacts on areas beyond the Mather South Project, under Regulation 18702.2(a)(7) there is a 
presumption that she will have a material financial interest unless there is clear and convincing 
evidence that the decision will not have any measurable impact on her real property. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
LKH:aja 
Sincerely 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
By: 
L. Karen Harrison 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

immediately adjacent to the General and Specific Plan boundary and over 13,000 feet from the 
Mather South Project boundary?

Based on the facts provided, the decisions solely affect the clearly defined Project area 
located over 13,000 feet from her property and within the larger Mather Field Specific Plan and 
General Plan areas. To the extent that the decisions to approve the Mather South Project, and the

South Urban Services Plan are solely limited in impact to the Mather South Project area, it is noreasonably foreseeable the decisions will have a material financial effect on Supervisor Peters' 
financial interest under Regulation 18702.2(b ).

The Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit any public official from making, 
participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental

Community Master Plan ("Mather South Project"), and accompanying amendments to the County's 
General Plan and Mather Specific Plan where she owns commercial and undeveloped real property 
immediately adjacent to the General and Specific Plan boundary and over 13,000 feet from the 
Mather South Project boundary?

located over 13,000 feet from her property and within the larger Mather Field Specific Plan and

financial interest under Regulation 18702.2(b ).
